title: Arenaria depauperata
taxonomy: Arenaria depauperata is classified within the plant kingdom as a member of the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the carnation or pink family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Caryophyllaceae family. Arenaria depauperata is a species of flowering plant within the genus Arenaria, which is a group of succulent plants. Within the Caryophyllaceae family, Arenaria depauperata is part of the tribe Caryophylleae. The classification of Arenaria depauperata is an important tool for botanists and researchers to understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.

intro: Arenaria depauperata, also known as the Least Sandwort, is a small, annual or biennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its tiny, white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues.
